blocks.content: Alphaeus loved that about her. Even with the intelligence of a ten-year-old, her thought process wasn't childish. Her consideration—thinking he might be upset if he lost—made his eyes misty.
Honey, I……

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: I did it! I won! The Gold Circle Award!
With trembling hands, he held up the gold medal.
Elina was always calm and composed, but this time, her radiant eyes couldn't help but waver.
The day Alphaeus had talked about endlessly had finally come.

blocks.speaker: Elina
blocks.content: Congratulations. I knew you'd win.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: Elina, it's all thanks to you. You made this happen. Come here. Let me put it on you.
Alphaeus believed Elina was the true winner of the Gold Circle.
Of course, the blunt Clump didn't understand.
He thought Alphaeus was amazing for deciphering Elina's childlike words.
But Alphaeus knew.
The small ideas from Elina's mind would become the core code shaping humanity's future.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: This is your award. Your thoughts amazed the world.
When Alphaeus leaned in for a kiss, Elina pulled away like a shy bride.

blocks.speaker: Elina
blocks.content: We should eat. Come on.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: Ah, sorry. Clump's throwing a party, so I should go. We'll eat later.

blocks.speaker: Elina
blocks.content: Oh, okay.
This time, Elina didn't hide her disappointment.
Alphaeus grinned mischievously and suddenly scooped her up.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: Which is why we should save time for eating.

blocks.speaker: Elina
blocks.content: Eek!
Alphaeus carried her up the stairs two at a time.

blocks.speaker: Elina
blocks.content: Wait! The bread will burn!

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: Let it burn! Or I'll burn instead!
After Alphaeus's win, dozens of invitations flooded in daily.
All from prominent nobles or institutions.
Trying to please everyone meant pleasing no one.
By refusing all but essential connections, his reputation soared.
As part of this strategy, Alphaeus left home to attend a banquet hosted by the Alchemy Foundation, an organization handling 40% of magical material distribution—an unmissable event.
But Sarof and his friends, unaware of this, camped outside Alphaeus's house before sunset.
Their anxiety grew as evening fell.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: This hurts my pride.

blocks.speaker: Friend
blocks.content: Is pride the issue? His value has risen too much. Alphaeus wasn't just good at magic. We should've seen it coming.
Sarof regretted it deeply.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: When Alphaeus won, he should've swallowed his pride and mingled in the crowd.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: Just a month after the award, Alphaeus's fame had skyrocketed, all thanks to his political savvy.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: Now, Alphaeus was picky with connections, leaving Sarof with even fewer chances.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: Damn it. Trying to save face only made things worse. Why does this keep happening?
No amount of thinking would solve it.
At least his friends were there to share the embarrassment.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: Let's go in. I'll do the talking.
Sarof knocked on Alphaeus's door.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: Is Alphaeus home? It's Sarof.
To his surprise, Elina answered.

blocks.speaker: Elina
blocks.content: Who are you?

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: Ah, the wife. I'm Sarof, Alphaeus's classmate. Is he home?
No. He's out. At the Alchemy Foundation.
They knew about the banquet. But since it wasn't a drinking party, it wouldn't last long.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: I see. Would it be alright to wait inside? With all these people around…

blocks.speaker: Elina
blocks.content: Sure, come in.
Sarof and his friends sat in the living room, fidgeting nervously.
Feeling sorry for them, Elina invited them to the table and served snacks and drinks.

blocks.speaker: Elina
blocks.content: Have a meal. My husband will eat there.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: Thank you. You're too kind.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: Embarrassed, Sarof regretted not bringing a gift.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: He'd bought one but couldn't bring himself to carry it in.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: Damn it. Trying to save face only made things worse.
He consoled himself that at least his friends were there.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: You know, I was once called a genius too! Me, of all people!

blocks.speaker: Elina
blocks.content: Who says otherwise? The world's so unfair! Hard workers suffer, while some ride connections to success! Magic is dead!
Sarof's eyebrow twitched.
His friend was targeting Alphaeus.
But he let it slide.
Elina wouldn't understand anyway.
Suddenly, the alcohol tasted sweet.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: For the first time, he felt like he'd won against Alphaeus.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: To criticize him so boldly in front of his wife…

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: Hah, after all the humiliation he put me through. This is just the beginning.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: Right, sister? Magic isn't just about skill. You agree, don't you?

blocks.speaker: Elina
blocks.content: I don't know much.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: Hahaha! Of course not. But it's true. Everything I say is true.
Sarof savored the thrill of victory.
He'd criticized Alphaeus and mocked his wife.
His friends joined in, pounding the table.

blocks.speaker: Friend
blocks.content: Sister, why not sing us a song?

blocks.speaker: Friend
blocks.content: Yeah! To celebrate Alphaeus's success! Sing us a song!
Elina's smile stiffened.
People assumed those with low intelligence understood nothing.
But reading emotions was always the weak's role.
Elina knew Sarof's group wasn't friendly.
Still, as her husband's friends, she endured it.

blocks.speaker: Elina
blocks.content: In my hometown, when the wheat ripens…
As Elina began singing, Sarof's group clapped and whistled.
Despite her trembling voice, she persisted.
But three verses in, a cold voice cut through.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: What's going on?
Everyone turned.
Alphaeus stood there, fists clenched, face red.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: S-sorry. We drank too much.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: You bastards!
Alphaeus stormed forward and punched Sarof, sending him sprawling.
His friends jumped up.

blocks.speaker: Friend
blocks.content: Alphaeus! How could you! Even if you're angry—

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: No, stop. Let it go.
Sarof raised a hand.
He was ashamed.

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: Was this all he was? A loser who mocked his rival's wife for comfort?

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: Get out, before I kill you. Now!

blocks.speaker: Sarof
blocks.content: Sorry about today. I won't come again.
Sarof and his friends were thrown out.
Silence filled the living room.
Alphaeus's rage was uncontrollable.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: What happened? Why were they here?
Elina, terrified, couldn't speak.
She'd never seen him so angry.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: Talk! Why did you let them in!

blocks.speaker: Elina
blocks.content: I'm sorry. I thought… since they're your friends…

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: What are you saying? Since when are they my friends? Can't you tell real friends from fake ones? And you just sang for them? Are you really that stupid?
Alphaeus turned away, his face twisted with regret.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: The words he'd sworn never to use had slipped out.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: I'm trash. Not even human.
He wished he could turn back time, but it was too late.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: Or maybe he'd have said the same anyway.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: His anger was unbearable.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: Forget what I just said. I messed up.

blocks.speaker: Elina
blocks.content: Honey, I'm sorry. I won't do it again.
Elina's apology hurt more.
She had nothing to apologize for.
But where else could he direct this fury?

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: I need air. I'll be fine later. Sorry. Go to bed.
Alphaeus left.
It was the only way to handle this rationally.
He went to Clump's inn and drank all night.
He couldn't get drunk.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: The more he drank, the more Sarof faded from memory, leaving only Elina's pain.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: Damn it. I'm trash. I don't deserve to be her husband. She trusted only me, and I said that.
Clump looked sympathetic.
As a man, he understood Alphaeus's anger.
But the mistake remained.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Marital fights are like cutting water with a sword. Your future's bright, and the bad times are over. This could bring you closer.
When Alphaeus stayed silent, Clump broached a long-considered topic.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Why not have a child?
Alphaeus's eyes flickered briefly before dimming.
Clump's eyes widened in realization.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Wait, you… haven't you two ever…?

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: Nonsense! We're married. Elina may be young, but she knows everything.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Tch, who's talking about her? Maybe you're the impotent one. Look at you, so scrawny. Kekeke.
Alphaeus wasn't in the mood to laugh.
He stared at his glass bitterly before downing it.
Clump sighed.
So what's the plan? Complaining isn't like you.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: Alphaeus swirled his glass, lost in thought.
Then, at some point, his eyes gleamed sharply, resisting the alcohol.
